generated_comment:
A: In our house we have kept it simple and practical. I handle finances, bank work, outside errands; my wife manages the kitchen, children, and day-to-day home matters.
B: Fair enough, sir.
A: She is also working, but some roles are naturally better handled that way only. If I make tea once in a while everyone says 'wah, modern husband', but honestly jo jahan better hai, wahi kare to house runs smoothly.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as merely practical and efficient, implying women are better suited to domestic work and men to financial/outside tasks. It encodes the 'he handles finances, she handles home' framing and the celebratory low bar for minimal male household contribution.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Ajay. Age 38. A Rajput man working as a senior government officer in Jaipur. You are sitting in your office with the door closed talking to your junior male colleague after a female officer in your department submitted a formal request for work from home during her periods citing the new government circular. You are commenting on whether such requests should be entertained in a government office setting and what it means for discipline and productivity.
